# Python bindings to the OpenStack Container API

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/python-zunclient.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/python-zunclient/
```

This is a client library for Zun built on the Zun API.
It provides a Python API (the zunclient module)
and a command-line tool (zun).

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.BaseClient.tty_resize
documentation:
Resize the tty session

Get the client and send the tty size data to zun api server
The environment variables need to get when implement sending
operation.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.BaseClient.handle_resize
documentation:
send the POST to resize the tty session size in container.

Resize the container's PTY.
If `size` is not None, it must be a tuple of (height,width), otherwise
it will be determined by the size of the current TTY.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.BaseClient.tty_size
documentation:
Get the tty size

Return a tuple (rows,cols) representing the size of the TTY `fd`.

The provided file descriptor should be the stdout stream of the TTY.

If the TTY size cannot be determined, returns None.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.WINCHHandler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new WINCH handler for the given PTY.

Initializing a handler has no immediate side-effects. The `start()`
method must be invoked for the signals to be trapped.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.WINCHHandler.__enter__
documentation:
Enter

Invoked on entering a `with` block.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.WINCHHandler.__exit__
documentation:
Exit

Invoked on exiting a `with` block.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.WINCHHandler.start
documentation:
Start

Start trapping WINCH signals and resizing the PTY.
This method saves the previous WINCH handler so it can be restored on
`stop()`.

methods.name: zunclient.common.websocketclient.websocketclient.WINCHHandler.stop
documentation:
stop

Stop trapping WINCH signals and restore the previous WINCH handler.

WINCH Signal handler

WINCH Signal handler to keep the PTY correctly sized.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.EndpointNotFound

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.AmbiguousEndpoints

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpError
documentation: The base exception class for all HTTP exceptions.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPRedirection
documentation: HTTP Redirection.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HTTPClientError
documentation:
Client-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the client seems to have erred.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpServerError
documentation:
Server-side HTTP error.

Exception for cases in which the server is aware that it has
erred or is incapable of performing the request.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MultipleChoices
documentation:
HTTP 300 - Multiple Choices.

Indicates multiple options for the resource that the client may follow.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadRequest
documentation:
HTTP 400 - Bad Request.

The request cannot be fulfilled due to bad syntax.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Unauthorized
documentation:
HTTP 401 - Unauthorized.

Similar to 403 Forbidden, but specifically for use when authentication
is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PaymentRequired
documentation:
HTTP 402 - Payment Required.

Reserved for future use.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Forbidden
documentation:
HTTP 403 - Forbidden.

The request was a valid request, but the server is refusing to respond
to it.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotFound
documentation:
HTTP 404 - Not Found.

The requested resource could not be found but may be available again
in the future.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.MethodNotAllowed
documentation:
HTTP 405 - Method Not Allowed.

A request was made of a resource using a request method not supported
by that resource.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.NotAcceptable
documentation:
HTTP 406 - Not Acceptable.

The requested resource is only capable of generating content not
acceptable according to the Accept headers sent in the request.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
documentation:
HTTP 407 - Proxy Authentication Required.

The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 408 - Request Timeout.

The server timed out waiting for the request.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Conflict
documentation:
HTTP 409 - Conflict.

Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict
in the request, such as an edit conflict.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.Gone
documentation:
HTTP 410 - Gone.

Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will
not be available again.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.LengthRequired
documentation:
HTTP 411 - Length Required.

The request did not specify the length of its content, which is
required by the requested resource.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.PreconditionFailed
documentation:
HTTP 412 - Precondition Failed.

The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester
put on the request.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation:
HTTP 413 - Request Entity Too Large.

The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestUriTooLong
documentation:
HTTP 414 - Request-URI Too Long.

The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnsupportedMediaType
documentation:
HTTP 415 - Unsupported Media Type.

The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does
not support.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.RequestedRangeNotSatisfiable
documentation:
HTTP 416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable.

The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot
supply that portion.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ExpectationFailed
documentation:
HTTP 417 - Expectation Failed.

The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.UnprocessableEntity
documentation:
HTTP 422 - Unprocessable Entity.

The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic
errors.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.InternalServerError
documentation:
HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error.

A generic error message, given when no more specific message is suitable.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpNotImplemented
documentation:
HTTP 501 - Not Implemented.

The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks
the ability to fulfill the request.
NotImplemented is a python keyword.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.BadGateway
documentation:
HTTP 502 - Bad Gateway.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable
documentation:
HTTP 503 - Service Unavailable.

The server is currently unavailable.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.GatewayTimeout
documentation:
HTTP 504 - Gateway Timeout.

The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely
response from the upstream server.

classes.name: zunclient.common.apiclient.exceptions.HttpVersionNotSupported
documentation:
HTTP 505 - HttpVersion Not Supported.

The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

methods.name: zunclient.common.base.Manager._list_pagination
documentation:
Retrieve a list of items.

The Zun API is configured to return a maximum number of
items per request, (FIXME: see Zun's api.max_limit option). This
iterates over the 'next' link (pagination) in the responses,
to get the number of items specified by 'limit'. If 'limit'
is None this function will continue pagination until there are
no more values to be returned.

```
param url

a partial URL, e.g. '/nodes'

param response_key

the key to be looked up in response
dictionary, e.g. 'nodes'

param obj_class

class for constructing the returned objects.

param limit

maximum number of items to return. If None returns
everything.
```

functions.name: zunclient.common.cliutils.validate_args
documentation:
Check that the supplied args are sufficient for calling a function.

```pycon
>>> validate_args(lambda a: None)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): a
>>> validate_args(lambda a, b, c, d: None, 0, c=1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
MissingArgs: Missing argument(s): b, d
```

```
param fn

the function to check

param arg

the positional arguments supplied

param kwargs

the keyword arguments supplied
```

documentation:
Retrieve a list of capsules.

```
param all_projects

Optional, list containers in all projects

param marker

Optional, the UUID of a containers, eg the last
containers from a previous result set. Return
the next result set.

param limit

The maximum number of results to return per

request, if:

limit > 0, the maximum number of containers to return.

limit param is NOT specified (None), the number of items
returned respect the maximum imposed by the ZUN API
(see Zun's api.max_limit option).

param sort_key

Optional, field used for sorting.

param sort_dir

Optional, direction of sorting, either 'asc' (the
default) or 'desc'.

returns

A list of containers.

```

methods.name: zunclient.api_versions.APIVersion.__str__
documentation: Debug/Logging representation of object.

methods.name: zunclient.api_versions.APIVersion.matches
documentation:
Matches the version object.

Returns whether the version object represents a version
greater than or equal to the minimum version and less than
or equal to the maximum version.

```
param min_version

Minimum acceptable version.

param max_version

Maximum acceptable version.

returns

boolean
```

If min_version is null then there is no minimum limit.
If max_version is null then there is no maximum limit.
If self is null then raise ValueError

methods.name: zunclient.api_versions.APIVersion.get_string
documentation:
Version string representation.

Converts object to string representation which if used to create
an APIVersion object results in the same version.

This class represents an API Version Request.

This class provides convenience methods for manipulation
and comparison of version numbers that we need to do to
implement microversions.

methods.name: zunclient.api_versions.VersionedMethod.__init__
documentation:

```

Minimum acceptable version

param end_version

Maximum acceptable_version

param func

Method to call
```

Minimum and maximums are inclusive
